news.title: AMD is taking advantage of Intel's weakness: Analyst on earnings
news.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock is falling in Tuesday's extended hours trading despite topping fourth quarter estimates on the top and bottom line, as well as outpacing revenue guidance forecasts. Benchmark Company managing director and senior semiconductor research analyst Cody Acree comes on Market Domination Overtime to elaborate on how AMD is able to "take advantage" of Intel's (INTC) recent weaknesss. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

news.title: AMD Forecast Disappoints as Investors Seek Bigger AI Payoff
news.summary: Advanced Micro Devices reported first-quarter sales will be roughly $9.8 billion, plus or minus $300 million, the company said in a statement Tuesday. Analysts had estimated $9.39 billion on average, but some projections topped $10 billion, according to data compiled by Bloomberg. Kunjan Sobhani has more on "Bloomberg The Close."

news.title: AMD (AMD) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
news.summary: Lisa Su: Thank you, Matt, and good afternoon to all those listening today.  2025 was a defining year for Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. With record revenue, net income, and free cash flow driven by broad-based demand for our high-performance computing and AI products.  Gained significant server and PC processor share, and rapidly scaled our data center AI business as Instinct and ROCm adoption increased with cloud, enterprise, and AI customers.

news.title: AMD stock falls despite topping Q4 earnings, guidance estimates
news.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is out with its fourth quarter results, beating Wall Street's expectations on adjusted earnings ($1.53 per share vs. estimates of $1.32) and revenue ($10.27 billion vs. estimates of $9.6 billion). While the AI chipmaker's first quarter revenue forecast also outpaced forecasts, the stock is falling in Tuesday's after-hours trading. Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley dives into AMD's earnings release. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

news.title: AMD forecasts sequential revenue decline, shares fall
news.summary: Advanced Micro Devices on Tuesday forecast a slight decline in first-quarter revenue despite an unexpected boost from sales of its AI chips to China, raising doubt about whether it has the supply-chain clout to challenge Nvidia in ​the booming AI market.  AMD said its forecast for the current quarter also includes approximately $100 million in sales of its MI308 ‌AI chips to China, after the company had excluded it from sales forecasts for the fourth quarter in November.  Shares fell more than 5% in extended trading, after investors tried ‌to dissect how the company performed without the sales to China.

news.title: AMD: Record Quarter, But Stock Plummets - A Strong Buy Opportunity?
news.summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. beat Q4 earnings as data center revenue jumped 39%, but shares fell on guidance and China export curbs. Click for this AMD update.

news.title: AMD: Strong Growth -- Shares Slump?
news.summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. beat Q4 revenue and EPS, led by 39% data center growth, but shares slipped on valuation and rising costs. Click for this AMD update.

news.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Beats Q4 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
news.summary: Advanced Micro (AMD) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of +16.18% and +6.24%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2025. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

news.title: GIGABYTE Partnership Expansion Across AI PCs and Displays Could Be A Game Changer For Advanced Micro Devices (AMD)
news.summary: In late January 2026, GIGABYTE said it was deepening its partnership with Advanced Micro Devices across AI gaming laptops, X870E X3D motherboards, and AMD FreeSync Premium Pro-certified OLED monitors to push on-device AI and smoother gaming performance. This expanded collaboration highlights how AMD is extending its AI platform beyond data centers into everyday consumer and creator hardware, potentially broadening its overall compute ecosystem.
